title: botrychium multifidum subsp. californicum
intro: Botrychium multifidum subsp. californicum, commonly known as the California grape fern, is a rare and endangered species of fern native to the western United States and Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of the California grape fern include reducing inflammation, soothing sore throats, and treating fever, as well as being used as a natural antiseptic to prevent infection. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
